summary:
The main reason for speech problems in cleft palate patients is tongue and soft palate dysfunction leading to improper sealing of the oral cavity during speech. This results from structural abnormalities preventing effective closure of the nasopharynx, causing air leakage into the nose and resulting in hypernasality or lisps. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{B}}$

Only summarize the agent’s actual reasoning—do not add external information or interpret beyond what was stated.

The main reason for speech problems in cleft palate patients is dysfunctional soft palate anatomy failing to seal the nasal passage effectively during speech. This leads directly to abnormal airflow through the nose, producing symptoms such as hypernasality and lisping.  

$\boxed{\text{B}}$
